mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: The Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that continuously scans your cluster for vulnerabilities, configuration issues, and compliance. It automatically generates security reports as Custom Resource Definitions, making it easy to monitor and manage security risks in your Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for resource allocation for scanning and potential performance impacts during high-load operations.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Aqua Security Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/trivy-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Trivy Operator Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the specified namespace, creating the necessary resources for operation.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.32.1

```

> Creates a namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the trivy-system namespace to verify that the Trivy Operator is running.

steps.title: Create Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to initiate a security scan.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: example-deployment
EOF

```

> Creates a sample VulnerabilityReport CR to trigger a scan.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: example-deployment
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all security reports and configurations related to the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace, which will also remove all resources in it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configurations and reports. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation afterward.

```bash

kubectl get all -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-backup.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.32.1

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backs up current configurations and reports.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running correctly.
commands: kubectl get all -n trivy-system -o yaml > trivy-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "VulnerabilityReport" in version "trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) for VulnerabilityReport may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Trivy Operator is installed correctly and the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'trivy-system' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or lacking necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' and ensure RBAC permissions are set correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Vulnerability reports are not being generated.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator may not be watching the correct namespaces or resources.

**Fix:**
Verify the operator's configuration and ensure it is set to watch the intended namespaces.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be down or unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your Kubernetes cluster and ensure network connectivity to the API server.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new compliance reports, including CIS Kubernetes Benchmark v1.23 and enhanced RBAC scanning capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system'.

kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:

    kind: Deployment
    name: example-deployment
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
